commits: ceffaad: docs(0.5.2): NAME-aliases implementation design memo
e5a7a80: test(0.5.2): RED — collision-safe alias resolution, shared classifier, catalog inference, batch-key + reference integrity
50d2920: feat(0.5.2): provider-prefixed model aliases + collision-safe resolution
68a49c0: fix(0.5.2): block ambiguous dropped bodies from fuzzy repoint (codex BLOCK)
479756f: chore(0.5.2): update closure artifact after codex-BLOCK fix
2d2bb80: fix(0.5.2): reset alias-table state before early returns (no stale reload)

tests.note: 1 xpassed — the pre-existing xfail tests/test_config_consistency.py::TestAliasTableCoverage::test_all_model_names_resolve_to_self (strict=False) now resolves correctly thanks to the collision-safe loader; reported as xpassed, not a failure.

blockers_encountered.description: [DETECT] Codex review found a real correctness bug: the two-pass loader correctly DROPS ambiguous variant keys (bodies claimed by >=2 entries under different providers) from _exact, but resolve() still leaked those dropped bodies into the fuzzy _score_match slow path. A NON-slash input like resolve('gemini-3.1-pro-preview') (served by BOTH gemini/ and vertex_ai/, no explicit alias equal to that body) was not in _exact, had no '/', fell to fuzzy, scored a ~1.0 exact-body match, and silently picked the FIRST entry (AI-Studio) and cached it — the exact silent cross-provider repoint the design said to prevent.
blockers_encountered.resolution: [RESOLVE] Added self._ambiguous_variants set (init in __init__, reset in load_from_config); pass 2 records every dropped multi-claimer variant key into it. resolve() now returns None (no fuzzy, no cache) for both the slash case (bare in _ambiguous_variants, checked after index/body/exact steps) and the non-slash case (lower in _ambiguous_variants, checked immediately before the fuzzy loop). RED-first regression added in tests/test_model_alias.py::TestAmbiguousBodyNoFuzzyRepoint (synthetic two-provider config + real-config gemini-3.1-pro-preview); confirmed RED then GREEN. Fixed in commit 68a49c0. Verified: resolve('gemini-3.1-pro-preview') -> None (uncached) while resolve('aistudio/gemini-3.1-pro')/resolve('vertex/gemini-3.1-pro') still resolve to their own entries.

blockers_encountered.description: [DETECT] Codex re-review concern: load_from_config reset table state (_entries/_exact/_provider_body_alias/_body_providers/_ambiguous_variants) only AFTER the early-return guards (missing file / YAML parse error / non-dict config). A reload over a now-missing or invalid config returned early and silently carried the STALE table from the previous successful load.
blockers_encountered.resolution: [RESOLVE] Moved all table-state resets to the TOP of load_from_config (before the path.is_file / try-except yaml.safe_load / isinstance(cfg, dict) guards), so every early return leaves a cleanly-empty table. self._loaded = True kept on the early returns; resolve() logic unchanged. RED-first regression tests/test_model_alias.py::TestModelAliasTable::test_reload_missing_config_clears_stale_table asserts an empty table + resolve()->None after reload at a non-existent path. Fixed in commit 2d2bb80.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Config anchors drifted as new entries were inserted (expected per brief); all edits were anchored on entry bodies, not line numbers, so no manual re-grep adjustment was needed.

NOTE for orchestrator: tests/test_config_consistency.py loads TWO configs — the existing TestAliasTableCoverage/TestFallbackConsistency suites read airlock/cli/templates/config.yaml (the simpler CLI template, NOT in this pack's MODIFY set, left untouched), while the NEW reference-integrity + batch-key tests read the deployed root config.yaml. The template is intentionally not updated here (out of scope); template-based tests still pass.

capability.py was kept minimal (airlock_provider_for + normalize_provider_token only). endpoints_for/capability_record/model_info shown in design note §4.5 are CAP-modelinfo's surface and were deliberately NOT added.

Post-review fixes: (1) self._ambiguous_variants + two None-guards in resolve() (codex-BLOCK); (2) moved table-state resets to the top of load_from_config so invalid/missing reloads cannot carry stale state (codex-CONCERN).
agent_verify_result: pass — targeted: 252 passed, 1 xpassed, 0 failed. Adjacent regression after the fixes (guardian/monitor/batch-gateway/enhanced): 135 passed. Full-suite has 1 pre-existing unrelated failure (tests/test_fathom_init.py::test_init_engine_with_fathomdb — fails by design because optional fathomdb is not installed; imports none of capability/model_alias/router).
next_step_for_orchestrator: Pack 0.5.2-NAME-aliases on branch feat/0.5.2-NAME-aliases at 2d2bb80d5f878289b7a8a34931296d8030397869 (code head); review, verify, merge. Optional follow-up: mirror the new prefixed aliases into airlock/cli/templates/config.yaml if the CLI template should advertise them (out of scope here).
